name: createFab
description:
Explicitly initializes the global `fab` object and returns it.

`createFab()` is optional: `fab` is created automatically before `setup()` runs.
Calling it multiple times returns the same instance, preserving any open
serial connection across reloads.

name: nozzleDiameter
description:
The printer's nozzle diameter in mm. Used to calculate extrusion amounts automatically.

You can configure the nozzle diameter via `setPrinter()`
or directly to override the preset.

name: filamentDiameter
description:
The filament diameter in mm. Used to calculate extrusion amounts automatically.
Set via `setPrinter()` or assign directly to override the preset.

name: maxX
description:
The maximum X travel distance of the printer in mm.
Set by the printer profile; assign directly to override.

name: maxY
description:
The maximum Y travel distance of the printer in mm.
Set by the printer profile; assign directly to override.

name: maxZ
description:
The maximum Z travel distance of the printer in mm.
Set by the printer profile; assign directly to override.

name: centerX
description: The X center of the build plate in mm (`maxX / 2`). Useful for centering prints.

name: centerY
description: The Y center of the build plate in mm (`maxY / 2`). Useful for centering prints.

name: x
description: Current planned X position in mm (local coordinates, before any `translate` offset).

name: y
description: Current planned Y position in mm (local coordinates, before any `translate` offset).

name: z
description: Current planned Z position in mm (local coordinates, before any `translate` offset).

name: isPrinting
description:
Whether a print is currently in progress.
Useful inside `draw()` to update a visualization or UI while printing.

name: commands
description:
The GCode commands generated by the most recent fabDraw() call, as structured objects. Each entry has `.command` (e.g. 'G1', 'M104'), `.fields` (all parameters as a map), and direct lowercase field access (`.x`, `.s`, `.f`, etc.). Cleared and rebuilt each time fabDraw() runs.

A line of GCode consists of fields that are separated by spaces.
A field can be interpreted as a command, a parameter, or some custom purpose. It typically consists of a letter directly followed by a number. For example, `G1` is a linear move command. For a comprehensive overview of GCode commands, see {@link https://marlinfw.org/meta/gcode/ Marlin's GCode dictionary}.

name: gcode
description:
All generated G-code as a single newline-separated string.
Useful for downloading or logging the full output of `fabDraw()`.

name: extrusionMultiplier
description:
Set the extrusion multiplier applied to all subsequent auto-calculated extrusion amounts.
Works like `strokeWeight` in p5.js — set once and it applies to all moves until changed.
Automatically resets to the printer's default value at the start of each `fabDraw()` call.
Use `push()` / `pop()` to scope a temporary change.

params.name: value
params.description: Multiplier value (e.g. `1.2` for 20% over-extrusion, `0.8` for bridging).

name: retractAmount
description:
Set the filament retraction distance for `retractTo()` and `retractBy()`.
Resets to the printer profile default at the start of each `fabDraw()` call.
Use `push()` / `pop()` to scope a temporary change.

name: zHopHeight
description:
Set the z-hop height for `retractTo()` and `retractBy()`.
Resets to the printer profile default at the start of each `fabDraw()` call.
Use `push()` / `pop()` to scope a temporary change.

name: push
description:
Begins a command group that contains its own printing state. By default, printing parameters (e.g., `extrusionMultiplier()`, `speed()`) and transformations (e.g., `translate()`) apply to all subsequent commands. `push()` and `pop()` can be used to constrain the effect of print parameters and transformations to a specific group of commands. The functionality follows `push()` and `pop()` in p5.js. `push()` and `pop()` contain the effects of the following functions:
  • `extrusionMultiplier()`
  • `speed()`
  • `translate()`
  • `retractAmount()`
  • `zHopHeight()`

name: pop
description:
Ends a command group that contains its own printing state. By default, printing parameters (e.g., `extrusionMultiplier()`, `speed()`) and transformations (e.g., `translate()`) apply to all subsequent commands. `push()` and `pop()` can be used to constrain the effect of print parameters and transformations to a specific group of commands. The functionality follows `push()` and `pop()` in p5.js. `push()` and `pop()` contain the effects of the following functions:
  • `extrusionMultiplier()`
  • `speed()`
  • `translate()`
  • `retractAmount()`
  • `zHopHeight()`

name: translate
description:
Offset the coordinate origin for all subsequent moves by `(dx, dy, dz)`.
Offsets accumulate — a second `translate` adds to the first.
Use `push()` / `pop()` to scope a translation to a specific region.

name: setPrinter
description: Configure the fab instance for a specific printer preset, with optional overrides.

params.name: overrides
params.description: Optional settings to override the preset (e.g. `{ nozzleDiameter: 0.4 }`).

name: render
description: Render a 3D preview of the planned toolpath. Call this inside a WEBGL p5.js `draw()` loop.

name: setCameraView
description: Snap the camera to an axis-aligned view.

name: autoHome
description: Home all axes and reset the extruder position.

name: setTemps
description: Set nozzle and bed temperatures and wait for both to be reached before continuing.

name: setNozzleTemp
description: Set the nozzle temperature and wait for it to be reached before continuing.

name: setBedTemp
description: Set the bed temperature and wait for it to be reached before continuing.

name: fanOn
description: Turn the part cooling fan on at full speed.

name: fanOff
description: Turn the part cooling fan off.

name: pausePrint
description: Pause the print for a given duration.

params.name: t
params.description: Duration in seconds. Defaults to 10s if not provided.

name: stopPrint
description: Immediately stop the print and clear the command queue.

name: introLine
description: Print a priming line along the left edge of the bed to prepare the extruder.

params.name: z
params.description: Layer height for the intro line in mm.

name: moveTo
description: Move to an absolute XYZ position without extruding.

params.name: comment
params.description: Optional G-code comment appended to the command.

name: move
description: Move relative to the current position without extruding.

params.name: dx
params.description: Distance to move in X in mm.

params.name: dy
params.description: Distance to move in Y in mm.

params.name: dz
params.description: Distance to move in Z in mm.

name: extrudeTo
description:
Move to an absolute XYZ position while extruding filament.
Extrusion amount is calculated automatically from the move distance, scaled by `extrusionMultiplier`.
Pass an explicit `e` value in mm to override the auto-calculation entirely.

params.name: e
params.description: Extrusion amount in mm. Calculated automatically if null.

name: extrude
description:
Move a relative distance in XYZ while extruding filament.
Extrusion amount is calculated automatically from the move distance, scaled by `extrusionMultiplier`.
Pass an explicit `e` value in mm to override the auto-calculation entirely.

params.name: dx
params.description: Distance to move in X in mm.

params.name: dy
params.description: Distance to move in Y in mm.

params.name: dz
params.description: Distance to move in Z in mm.

params.name: e
params.description: Extrusion amount in mm. Calculated automatically if null.

name: retractTo
description:
Travel to an absolute XYZ position with a filament retraction, z-hop, and re-prime.
Use this to travel between disconnected extrusion paths without stringing.
Retract distance and z-hop height are set by `retractAmount()` and `zHopHeight()`.

name: retractBy
description:
Travel a relative XYZ distance with a filament retraction, z-hop, and re-prime.
Use this to travel between disconnected extrusion paths without stringing.
Retract distance and z-hop height are set by `retractAmount()` and `zHopHeight()`.

params.name: dx
params.description: Distance to move in X in mm.

params.name: dy
params.description: Distance to move in Y in mm.

params.name: dz
params.description: Distance to move in Z in mm.

name: travelTo
description: Move to an absolute position with a 2mm z-hop over the travel path.

name: moveToX
description: Move to an absolute X position without extruding.

name: moveToY
description: Move to an absolute Y position without extruding.

name: moveToZ
description: Move to an absolute Z position without extruding.

name: moveToE
description: Move the extruder to an absolute E position.

name: moveX
description: Move a relative distance in X without extruding.

name: moveY
description: Move a relative distance in Y without extruding.

name: moveZ
description: Move a relative distance in Z without extruding.

name: moveE
description: Move the extruder a relative distance in E.

name: extrudeX
description: Move a relative distance in X while extruding.

params.name: e
params.description: Extrusion amount in mm. Calculated automatically if null.

name: extrudeXY
description: Move a relative distance in X and Y while extruding.

params.name: dx
params.description: Distance to move in X in mm.

params.name: dy
params.description: Distance to move in Y in mm.

params.name: e
params.description: Extrusion amount in mm. Calculated automatically if null.

name: extrudeY
description: Move a relative distance in Y while extruding.

params.name: e
params.description: Extrusion amount in mm. Calculated automatically if null.

name: extrudeZ
description: Move a relative distance in Z while extruding.

params.name: e
params.description: Extrusion amount in mm. Calculated automatically if null.

name: extrudeToX
description: Move to an absolute X position while extruding.

params.name: e
params.description: Extrusion amount in mm. Calculated automatically if null.

name: extrudeToY
description: Move to an absolute Y position while extruding.

params.name: e
params.description: Extrusion amount in mm. Calculated automatically if null.

name: extrudeToXY
description: Move to an absolute XY position while extruding.

params.name: e
params.description: Extrusion amount in mm. Calculated automatically if null.

name: extrudeToZ
description: Move to an absolute Z position while extruding.

params.name: e
params.description: Extrusion amount in mm. Calculated automatically if null.

name: circle
description: Extrude a circle centered at (x, y) at the given Z height.

params.name: e
params.description: Extrusion amount per segment. Calculated automatically if null.

name: speed
description: Set the feedrate for subsequent moves. Persistent — applies to all moves until changed, like `strokeWeight()` in p5.js.

name: prime
description: Prime the extruder by pushing filament forward. Used after a retraction.

name: setMaxAcceleration
description: Set the maximum acceleration for each axis.

name: setStartAcceleration
description: Set the starting acceleration for print moves.

name: makeE
description:
Calculate the extrusion amount needed to move to an absolute XYZ position
based on the current nozzle and filament diameters.

returns.description: Extrusion amount in mm, fixed to 4 decimal places.

name: pickupTool
description: Select a tool by index (for multi-tool machines like Jubilee).

name: addComment
description: Append a comment to the last command in the queue.

name: fabDraw
description:
A function called once to generate G-code commands.

p5.fab calls `fabDraw()` automatically once after `setup()` completes and before `draw()` runs, and again whenever `stopPrint()` is called to regenerate the command queue.

name: setup
description: A p5.js function that's called once when the sketch begins running. See the p5.js reference for more information. In the copypastes.xyz editor, `setup()` re-runs every time you run your sketch. This means changes to `setup()` (e.g. `setPrinter()`, canvas size) take effect immediately.

name: draw
description: A p5.js function that's repeatedly called while the sketch runs. See the p5.js reference for more information. p5.fab uses the `draw()` loop to `render()` a visualization of the machine and toolpaths written in the `fabDraw()` function. Additional elements can be drawn as desired.
